_metadata.description: Beast Era characters — NA Beast Wars (S1-S3), Beast Machines, Beast Wars II, Beast Wars Neo, and toy-only characters

characters.name: Optimus Primal
characters.notes: Maximal commander. Descendant of Optimus Prime. Voiced by Gary Chalk. Upgraded to Transmetal in S2, then Optimal Optimus in S3. Central character in both Beast Wars and Beast Machines

characters.name: Megatron
characters.notes: Predacon leader. Named after G1 Megatron but a different character. Voiced by David Kaye. Upgraded to Transmetal in S2, then dragon Transmetal 2 in S3. Main antagonist of Beast Machines

characters.name: Cheetor
characters.notes: Young Maximal scout. Voiced by Ian James Corlett. Upgraded to Transmetal in S2, then Transmetal 2 in S3. Becomes a leader in Beast Machines

characters.name: Rattrap
characters.notes: Maximal spy and demolitions expert. Voiced by Scott McNeil. Upgraded to Transmetal in S2. Appears in Beast Machines as techno-organic reformatted form

characters.name: Rhinox
characters.notes: Maximal scientist and warrior. Voiced by Richard Newman. Upgraded to Transmetal in S2. In Beast Machines, becomes Vehicon general Tankor against his will

characters.name: Dinobot
characters.notes: Former Predacon who defects to the Maximals in S1E1. Warrior of honor. Voiced by Scott McNeil. Dies heroically in S2 episode 'Code of Hero'. One of the most beloved TF characters

characters.name: Tarantulas
characters.notes: Predacon scientist and schemer. Voiced by Alec Willows. Secretly a Tripredacus agent. Upgraded to Transmetal in S2. Killed by Tigerhawk in S3 finale

characters.name: Waspinator
characters.notes: Predacon aerial warrior. Voiced by Scott McNeil. Fan-favorite comic relief, constantly destroyed and rebuilt. Upgraded to Transmetal in S2. Sole Predacon survivor of Beast Wars. Briefly appears in Beast Machines

characters.name: Terrorsaur
characters.notes: Predacon aerial warrior. Voiced by Doug Parker. Frequent mutineer against Megatron. Destroyed by quantum surge at start of S2

characters.name: Scorponok
characters.notes: Predacon second-in-command. Different character from G1 Scorponok. Voiced by Don Brown. Loyal to Megatron. Destroyed by quantum surge at start of S2

characters.name: Blackarachnia
characters.notes: Predacon who gradually shifts allegiance to Maximals. Voiced by Venus Terzo. Originally a Maximal protoform reprogrammed by Tarantulas. Becomes Transmetal 2 in S3. Major character in Beast Machines

characters.name: Tigatron
characters.notes: Maximal scout. Voiced by Blu Mankuma. Prefers nature over technology. Romantic relationship with Airazor. Both abducted by Vok aliens in S1 finale. Returns merged as Tigerhawk in S3

characters.name: Airazor
characters.notes: Maximal aerial scout. Voiced by Pauline Newstone. Born on prehistoric Earth from a stasis pod. Romantic relationship with Tigatron. Abducted by Vok in S1 finale

characters.name: Inferno
characters.notes: Predacon warrior with ant programming that makes him fanatically loyal to Megatron (whom he calls 'the Royalty'). Different character from G1 Inferno. Voiced by Jim Byrnes. Destroyed in S3 finale

characters.name: Silverbolt
characters.notes: Noble Maximal Fuzor. Different character from G1 Silverbolt. Voiced by Scott McNeil. Romantic relationship with Blackarachnia. In Beast Machines, reformatted as Vehicon general Jetstorm

characters.name: Quickstrike
characters.notes: Predacon Fuzor. Crude and aggressive. Voiced by Colin Murdock. Destroyed in S3 finale

characters.name: Depth Charge
characters.notes: Maximal security officer obsessed with hunting Rampage. Voiced by David Sobolov. Transmetal. Both he and Rampage die in mutual destruction in S3 finale

characters.name: Rampage
characters.notes: Predacon berserker with an indestructible spark (Starscream's mutant clone experiment). Different character from G1 Rampage. Voiced by Campbell Lane. Transmetal. Destroyed by Depth Charge in S3 finale

characters.name: Tigerhawk
characters.notes: Vok emissary created by merging the sparks of Tigatron and Airazor. Voiced by Blu Mankuma. Extremely powerful. Destroyed by Megatron's Nemesis battleship in S3 finale

characters.name: Dinobot II
characters.notes: Clone of original Dinobot created by Megatron from Rampage's spark fragment. Transmetal 2. Initially mindless, eventually regains original Dinobot's sense of honor in S3 finale

characters.name: Optimal Optimus
characters.notes: Optimus Primal's ultimate form after merging with Optimus Prime's spark. Super class toy. Four alternate modes. Appeared in S3 premiere

characters.name: Magnaboss
characters.notes: Combined form of Ironhide (BW), Prowl (BW), and Silverbolt (BW). Toy-only combiner, not in the TV show. Each component is a different animal

characters.name: Ironhide
characters.notes: Magnaboss component. Beast Wars toy-only character, not in the TV show. Different character from G1 Ironhide

characters.name: Prowl
characters.notes: Magnaboss component. Beast Wars toy-only character, not in the TV show. Different character from G1 Prowl

characters.name: Silverbolt (Magnaboss)
characters.notes: Magnaboss component torso. Beast Wars toy-only character. Different from the Fuzor Silverbolt (BW) and G1 Silverbolt

characters.name: Tripredacus
characters.notes: Combined form of Cicadacon, Ram Horn, and Sea Clamp. Toy-only combiner, not in the TV show. The Tripredacus Council appears in the show but not the combined form

characters.name: Cicadacon
characters.notes: Tripredacus component. Member of the Tripredacus Council. Appears briefly in BW S2 episode 'The Agenda'

characters.name: Ram Horn
characters.notes: Tripredacus component. Member of the Tripredacus Council. Appears briefly in BW S2 episode 'The Agenda'

characters.name: Sea Clamp
characters.notes: Tripredacus component. Member of the Tripredacus Council. Appears briefly in BW S2 episode 'The Agenda'

characters.name: Polar Claw
characters.notes: Maximal tracker. Toy-only character, not in the TV show. Mega class figure

characters.name: Wolfang
characters.notes: Maximal warrior. Toy-only character, not in the TV show. Deluxe class figure

characters.name: Buzz Saw
characters.notes: Predacon warrior. Toy-only character, not in the TV show. Deluxe class figure. Not to be confused with G1 Buzzsaw cassette

characters.name: Razorbeast
characters.notes: Maximal warrior. Toy-only in the TV show but major character in IDW Beast Wars comics. Basic class figure

characters.name: Armordillo
characters.notes: Maximal warrior. Toy-only character, not in the TV show. Basic class figure

characters.name: Insecticon
characters.notes: Predacon warrior. Toy-only character, not in the TV show. Basic class figure. Name reused from G1 Insecticons sub-group

characters.name: Snapper
characters.notes: Predacon warrior. Toy-only character, not in the TV show. Basic class figure

characters.name: Iguanus
characters.notes: Predacon warrior. Toy-only character in BW (G1 Iguanus was a Pretender). Basic class figure. Name reused from G1

characters.name: Cybershark
characters.notes: Maximal warrior. Toy-only character, not in the TV show. Deluxe class figure

characters.name: Manterror
characters.notes: Predacon warrior. Toy-only character, not in the TV show. Deluxe class figure

characters.name: B'Boom
characters.notes: Maximal warrior. Toy-only character, not in the TV show. Mega class figure

characters.name: Transquito
characters.notes: Predacon warrior. Toy-only character, not in the TV show. Mega class figure

characters.name: Nightscream
characters.notes: Beast Machines Maximal. Young bot who survived Megatron's conquest of Cybertron. Voiced by Alessandro Juliani. Born on Cybertron, never a Beast Wars combatant

characters.name: Botanica
characters.notes: Beast Machines Maximal. Plant-based transformer. Voiced by Kathleen Barr. Joins the team in S2. Romantic interest of Rattrap

characters.name: Jetstorm
characters.notes: Beast Machines Vehicon general commanding aero-drones. Actually Silverbolt's spark reformatted by Megatron. Voiced by Brian Drummond. Eventually freed and returns to Maximal side

characters.name: Tankor
characters.notes: Beast Machines Vehicon general commanding tank drones. Actually Rhinox's spark reformatted by Megatron. Voiced by Paul Dobson. Develops independent agenda, destroyed in S1 finale

characters.name: Thrust
characters.notes: Beast Machines Vehicon general commanding cycle-drones. Different character from G1 Thrust. Voiced by Jim Byrnes. Actually Waspinator's spark reformatted

characters.name: Obsidian
characters.notes: Beast Machines Vehicon general commanding copter-drones. Ancient Cybertronian military strategist. Voiced by Paul Dobson. Partner of Strika. Loyal to Cybertron itself, not any faction

characters.name: Strika
characters.notes: Beast Machines Vehicon general. Ancient Cybertronian warrior. Voiced by Patricia Drake. Partner of Obsidian. One of the few female Vehicons

characters.name: Diagnostic Drone
characters.notes: Beast Machines. Megatron's diagnostic assistant. Non-sentient drone repurposed for various tasks. Gains brief sentience in one episode

characters.name: Noble
characters.notes: Beast Machines. Savage/Noble is a beast with two forms — wolf (Noble) and dragon (Savage). Contains Megatron's spark for a time. Non-speaking animal intelligence

characters.name: Razorclaw
characters.notes: Predacon warrior. Toy-only character. Basic class figure. Name reused from G1 Predacons leader

characters.name: Claw Jaw
characters.notes: Maximal warrior. Toy-only character, not in the TV show. Basic class figure

characters.name: Bonecrusher
characters.notes: Maximal warrior. Toy-only character. Deluxe class figure. Name reused from G1 Constructicon

characters.name: Grimlock
characters.notes: Maximal warrior. Toy-only character. Deluxe class figure. Name reused from G1 Dinobot leader. Different beast mode (velociraptor vs T-rex)

characters.name: Lio Convoy
characters.notes: Supreme Commander of the Maximals on planet Gaia. Scanned a white lion form. Gruff military exterior masking a compassionate nature. Strained relationship with his 'son' Lio Junior. Later promoted to Great Convoy rank after Cybertron's restoration

characters.name: Apache
characters.notes: Lio Convoy's loyal second-in-command. Transforms into a mandrill. Experienced soldier and strategist who supports Lio Convoy's leadership on Gaia

characters.name: Bighorn
characters.notes: Fierce Maximal warrior who transforms into a bison. Violent and rampaging in battle, but also a poet and naturalist in his downtime. Has an unrequited infatuation with the Seacon Scylla

characters.name: Tasmania Kid
characters.notes: Young and reckless Maximal recruit. Accidentally scanned a Tasmanian devil instead of his intended eagle form. Mischievous and insubordinate but shows growth through the series. Best friends with Diver

characters.name: Diver
characters.notes: Timid and laid-back Maximal who transforms into a giant frog. Prefers napping in nature but shows courage when friends are in danger. Best friends with Tasmania Kid. Valuable for aquatic operations

characters.name: Scuba
characters.notes: Quiet and solitary Maximal who transforms into a squid. Studies martial arts, poetry, and military history. Operates the Tako Tank amphibious vehicle. Unwilling object of Seacon Scylla's romantic pursuit. Cousin of Ikard

characters.name: Lio Junior
characters.notes: Child Transformer born from Angolmois Energy and Lio Convoy's Energon Matrix. Naive and immature but possesses significant combat abilities. Desperately seeks Lio Convoy's paternal approval. Central component of Magnaboss (BW II)

characters.name: Skywarp
characters.notes: Decorated Maximal teacher from Cybertron who transforms into a bald eagle. Dignified but not stuffy. Aerial combat and electronics expert. Mentors Lio Junior alongside Santon. Different character from G1 Skywarp

characters.name: Santon
characters.notes: Massive Maximal medic and staunch pacifist who transforms into an African elephant. Refuses to kill under any circumstance. Arrived on Gaia with Skywarp to warn about Nemesis. Mentors Lio Junior

characters.name: Magnaboss (Beast Wars II)
characters.notes: BW II combined form of Lio Junior, Skywarp, and Santon. Different composition from the Western Magnaboss (Ironhide/Prowl/Silverbolt). Lio Junior forms the chest and head

characters.name: Moon
characters.notes: Gaian monitor android with a rabbit form. Carefree and childish personality. Left behind by ancient Gaians to monitor Angolmois Energy corruption. Has flight and invisibility powers. Provides commentary alongside Artemis from the Moon

characters.name: Artemis
characters.notes: Gaian monitor android. Companion to Moon, left behind by ancient Gaians to monitor planet Gaia from the Moon. Provides episode commentary. More mature personality than Moon

characters.name: Galvatron
characters.notes: Self-proclaimed Emperor of Destruction and Predacon leader on Gaia. Triple Changer with dragon and drill tank modes. Seeks to harness Angolmois Energy. Unstable personality when overcharged. Prioritizes loyalty to his brother Megastorm despite the latter's constant betrayal attempts

characters.name: Megastorm
characters.notes: Duke of Destruction and Galvatron's younger brother. Transforms into a tank with a slow-charging Mega-Cannon. Constantly schemes to overthrow his brother while maintaining a facade of loyalty. Later upgraded to Gigastorm via Angolmois Energy

characters.name: Gigastorm
characters.notes: Megastorm's upgraded form after Angolmois Energy mutation. Arch-Duke of Destruction with dinosaur, base, and flying fortress modes. More powerful but still second-in-command to Galvatron. Has a Gigascouter drone companion

characters.name: Starscream
characters.notes: Galvatron's de facto second-in-command. Confident, clever, narcissistic, and effeminate. Avoids getting his hands dirty. Partners with BB for Combination Stealth Mode. Later upgraded to Hellscream via Angolmois Energy. Different character from G1 Starscream

characters.name: Hellscream
characters.notes: Starscream's Cyborg Beast upgrade after Angolmois Energy exposure. Shark beast mode with launchable head drone (Peeping Head) and Gale Dagger weapon. More powerful combat form

characters.name: BB
characters.notes: Intellectually limited Predacon whose vocabulary consists almost entirely of 'Roger.' Serves as brawn to Starscream's brains. Massive carpet-bombing firepower. Can combine with Starscream for Combination Stealth Mode. Later upgraded to Max-B

characters.name: Max-B
characters.notes: BB's Cyborg Beast upgrade after falling into Angolmois Energy. Flying cybernetic dog form. Enhanced combat strength but unchanged low intellect — vocabulary expanded only to include 'Max' alongside 'Roger'

characters.name: Dirge
characters.notes: Predacon Combatron jet. Inseparable partner and best friend of Thrust. The straight man in their manzai comedy duo. Also a Cybertronian gourmet skilled at enhancing high-energy fuels. Later upgraded to Dirgegun

characters.name: Dirgegun
characters.notes: Dirge's Cyborg Beast upgrade after Gigastorm fed Angolmois Energy through him. Transforms into a cyborg bee. Equipped with devastating Blast Leader ultrasonic attack

characters.name: Thrust (Beast Wars II)
characters.notes: Predacon Combatron jet. The comedian in the manzai duo with Dirge. Shrewd beneath his humor but frequently bungles missions. Hates the Maximal Scuba due to sea breezes causing rust. Later upgraded to Thrustor

characters.name: Thrustor
characters.notes: Thrust's Cyborg Beast upgrade after pledging loyalty to Gigastorm and receiving Angolmois Energy. All-around superior warrior who can no longer be laughed off the battlefield. Retains earlier character flaws

characters.name: Autostinger
characters.notes: Leader of the Autorollers mercenary team. More opportunistic than bloodthirsty, willing to grant mercy to place enemies in his debt. Eventually becomes indebted to Galvatron after being spared from execution

characters.name: Autocrusher
characters.notes: Oldest of the Autorollers. Relies on brute force, plowing through foes like a juggernaut. Rival of the Insectron Powerhug in strength contests

characters.name: Autojetter
characters.notes: Youngest Autoroller. Brash and undisciplined, prefers combat over bodyguard duties. Hates the Insectron Tonbot because dragonflies clog his jet intakes

characters.name: Autolauncher
characters.notes: Autoroller who yearns to be first at everything. Rushes ahead of teammates, serving as a useful distraction. Competes with the Insectron Mantis in the discipline of Discmaster

characters.name: Halfshell
characters.notes: Leader of the Space Pirate Seacons. Shrewd and calculating with money as primary motivation, but takes friendship seriously. Forms the torso of God Neptune

characters.name: Sea Phantom
characters.notes: Space Pirate Seacon. Stealthy aquatic warrior who forms the right arm of God Neptune

characters.name: Terrormander
characters.notes: Space Pirate Seacon. Fierce warrior who forms the right leg of God Neptune

characters.name: Coelagon
characters.notes: Space Pirate Seacon. Aquatic warrior who forms the left arm of God Neptune

characters.name: Scylla
characters.notes: Space Pirate Seacon and the team's sole female member. Flighty and often abandons her position in the God Neptune combination to pursue her own interests, leaving the combiner hopping on one leg. Object of Bighorn's unrequited affection and pursuer of Scuba

characters.name: God Neptune
characters.notes: Combined form of the Space Pirate Seacons: Halfshell, Sea Phantom, Terrormander, Coelagon, and Scylla. Wields the Neptune Sword. Often weakened when Scylla abandons her leg position mid-combination

characters.name: Majin Zarak
characters.notes: Giant Predacon fortress resembling Scorponok/Black Zarak. Appears in the final arc of Beast Wars II as Galvatron's ultimate weapon. Contains vast reserves of Angolmois Energy

characters.name: Ikard
characters.notes: Maximal courier and Scuba's cousin. Nasally-voiced know-it-all who runs a delivery service. Terrible pilot who crashes at most destinations. Delivered the Tako Tank to Scuba on Gaia

characters.name: NAVI
characters.notes: Maximal base computer on Gaia. Provides tactical information, communications, and mission support to Lio Convoy's crew

characters.name: Big Convoy
characters.notes: Gruff, solitary Maximal commander assigned by Vector Sigma to train a group of rookies. Initially a one-man army who distrusts teamwork, he gradually learns the value of cooperation. Wields the powerful Big Cannon

characters.name: Longrack
characters.notes: Uptight Maximal sub-commander and giraffe Transformer. Serves as Big Convoy's second-in-command among the rookie team. Frequently clashes with the rebellious Colada

characters.name: Colada
characters.notes: Arrogant Maximal recruit who views teammates as inferiors and resists authority. The most problematic of the new recruits, yet possesses genuine combat prowess. Wields a shockwave-creating whip

characters.name: Stampy
characters.notes: Young, cheerful but cowardly Maximal recruit. Despite his fearfulness, he serves as an excellent scout due to his speed and extremely sensitive ears. Can assume a Scissor Mode with diamond-sharp blades

characters.name: Break
characters.notes: Enthusiastic Maximal recruit with a hot-blooded personality who aspires to become Supreme Commander. Overflowing with infectious enthusiasm but can be obnoxious. Has a hidden connection to Predacons revealed mid-series

characters.name: Heinrad
characters.notes: Relaxed and unmotivated Maximal recruit who possesses the rare ability to momentarily freeze time. Prefers drinking oil and napping to working. Uses his time-stopping powers to play pranks on Predacons

characters.name: Mach Kick
characters.notes: Free-spirited Maximal with a rowdy attitude and genuine military experience. Despite his carefree party-loving demeanor, he is an accomplished and dependable warrior. Greatly respects Big Convoy

characters.name: Rockbuster
characters.notes: Solitary Maximal warrior and Big Convoy's former partner. Chose to fight Predacons as a vigilante soldier. Heavily armored and formidable enough to take on entire platoons alone

characters.name: Randy
characters.notes: Aggressive and dedicated Maximal powerhouse. Honest to a fault, often blurting out uncomfortable truths. Prefers working independently and has a strong dislike for underhanded tactics

characters.name: Sharp Edge
characters.notes: Unpredictable Maximal who appears calm on the surface but can erupt into destructive force at any moment. Views combat as his life's purpose and fears becoming obsolete if peace arrives

characters.name: Magmatron
characters.notes: Predacon leader who possesses a unique anatomy allowing him to separate into three dinosaur forms (Landsaurus, Skysaurus, Seasaurus) or merge into Magmatron. Seeks the title of Emperor of Destruction. Eventually allies with Maximals against Unicron, sacrificing himself in the final battle

characters.name: Landsaurus
characters.notes: Magmatron's land-based dinosaur component. Transforms into a Giganotosaurus, forming the main body of the combined Magmatron form

characters.name: Skysaurus
characters.notes: Magmatron's aerial dinosaur component. Transforms into a Quetzalcoatlus pterosaur, forming the upper body and wings of the combined Magmatron form

characters.name: Seasaurus
characters.notes: Magmatron's aquatic dinosaur component. Transforms into an Elasmosaurus, forming the tail and back-mounted weapon of the combined Magmatron form

characters.name: Guiledart
characters.notes: Loyal Predacon field commander who serves Magmatron faithfully. Unlike typical scheming Predacons, he seeks advancement through honorable means. In combat, he is ferocious and willing to play dirty, wielding Thunder Horn blasts

characters.name: Saberback
characters.notes: Enigmatic Predacon who is a cunning charlatan and compulsive liar. Prefers to bury himself underground and lure enemies with his Claw Trap Mode rather than fight directly. Enjoys teasing the less intelligent Sling

characters.name: Sling
characters.notes: Naive and dim Predacon who is the butt of everyone's jokes, including his own teammates. Despite being mistreated, he is enthusiastic and eager to please. Wields a multi-use Tail Bunker weapon and can disguise as a flower

characters.name: Dead End
characters.notes: Ideal Predacon soldier, content in his rank and never scheming for advancement. Prefers solitary work and expresses cruelty independently. Wields the Spiral Bomb and Dead Gun weapons

characters.name: Hydra
characters.notes: Desperate Predacon strategist seeking recognition from Magmatron. The fastest Predacon alive with keen tactical cleverness, but overlooked in favor of rival Guiledart. Stationed alone at the remote Porcupine outpost

characters.name: Crazybolt
characters.notes: Neither fast nor strong Predacon who is usually the first to flee from danger. An habitual liar who tells falsehoods as second nature, but his reputation means no one believes him even when truthful. Design references the Jurassic Park Dilophosaurus

characters.name: Archadis
characters.notes: Arrogant Predacon marksman hailed by Magmatron to counter the Maximals. An adept sharpshooter with genuine combat skills and flight capability. Self-centered and willing to betray allies for personal gain

characters.name: Unicron
characters.notes: The cosmic devil, resurrected by the Blentrons as an energy being in Beast Wars Neo. Assumes Galvatron's appearance. Serves as the ultimate antagonist of the series. Absorbs the Blentrons to sustain himself in the final battle against Big Convoy

characters.name: Latolata
characters.notes: De facto leader of the Blentrons, Unicron's servants. Freakishly powerful, able to defeat both Big Convoy and Magmatron with little effort. Can regenerate from nearly any damage. Ultimately absorbed by Unicron

characters.name: Elphaorpha
characters.notes: Blentron who functions as the team's galactic transport. Devoted servant of Unicron with extraordinary power. Can shrug off nearly any attack. Ultimately absorbed by Unicron

characters.name: Drancron
characters.notes: Blentron assassin capable of slicing opponents apart. Devoted servant of Unicron. Nearly invincible in combat. Ultimately absorbed by Unicron in the final confrontation

characters.name: Great Convoy
characters.notes: Superior Maximal officer in Beast Wars Neo. Oversees Big Convoy's mission and the overall Maximal command structure
